title: Setup and Operation of Single Use Bubble Column Reactors for Recombinant Proteins
author: Nathan Wright
materials: Allow incubator to reach set temperature.
Flash freeze the cell pellet in liquid nitrogen.

steps.title: Prepare and autoclaved Terrific Broth Base...
steps.instruction: Prepare and autoclaved Terrific Broth Base including Trace elements (Formedium AIMTB310)

steps.title: Add 1 L, prepared AIM TB...
steps.instruction: Add 1 L, prepared AIM TB media to each BCR bag.

steps.title: Transfer BCR bags to incubator
steps.instruction: Transfer BCR bags to incubator.

steps.title: Attach sparger stones to air control valves
steps.instruction: Attach sparger stones to air control valves. Set air flow, 1 lpm.

steps.title: Add 10 mL, starter culture, per L main culture
steps.instruction: Add 10 mL, starter culture, per L main culture.

steps.title: Continue incubation 37 °C , 04:00:00 (approximately)
steps.instruction: Continue incubation 37 °C , 04:00:00 (approximately). Incubation at 37 °C should continue until the culture has entered the exponential growth phase. Typically this takes 4-6 hours given the conditions described. Autoinduction media will trigger protein expression once all the glucose in the media has been consumed, this happens at OD600 of between 3-5 in most cases. The incubation temperature should be reduced before the autoinduction point to promote protein solubility.

steps.title: For protein expression we used the following plasmids
steps.instruction: For protein expression we used the following plasmids: Zika virus portion of NS2B protease coexpressed with NS3 protease addgene Catalog #228663

steps.title: Incubate (ambient) Overnight (approximately 16 h)...
steps.instruction: Incubate (ambient) Overnight (approximately 16 h) [EndPoint] ΔOD600≈0 Without external cooling cultures' metabolic activity will heat the culture to 25-27 °C . If working with proteins with temperature sensitive expression, an external recirculating chiller can be connected to the water bath.

steps.title: To each litre of sterilised media
steps.instruction: To each litre of sterilised media: Add 1 mL, 10% Antifoam 204 Add 1 mL, 1000x antibiotic stock (e.g. Kanamycin 50mg/ml) Add 20 g, glycerol

steps.title: Harvest:
steps.instruction: Harvest:

steps.title: Loading and equilibrating the BCR bags:
steps.instruction: Loading and equilibrating the BCR bags:

steps.title: Insert sparger stones into BCR bag,...
steps.instruction: Insert sparger stones into BCR bag, making sure they are at the bottom of the BCR.

steps.title: Take final OD600 reading
steps.instruction: Take final OD600 reading

steps.title: Collect 1 mL, culture samples for diagnostic testing
steps.instruction: Collect 1 mL, culture samples for diagnostic testing

steps.title: Transfer culture to 1 L centrifuge...
steps.instruction: Transfer culture to 1 L centrifuge bottles The Bags are long and flexible, decanting by pouring culture out from the top of bags can lead to spillage. Place Bags into a large jug or similar. With a sharp implement, make a small incision in the bag and allow it to drain into the jug. There is a lot of hydrostatic pressure on the liquid at the bottom of the Bags when full. To avoid splashing make incisions towards the top of the liquid level at first. As the culture drains, larger incisions can be made at the bottom to drain all liquid and sediments.

steps.title: Pellet the cell, 4000 rcf, 4 °C, 30 minutes
steps.instruction: Pellet the cell, 4000 rcf, 4 °C, 30 minutes

steps.title: Drain spent media and sterilise with 1 tablet/L Virkon
steps.instruction: Drain spent media and sterilise with 1 tablet/L Virkon

steps.title: Record wet cell weight (g)
steps.instruction: Record wet cell weight (g)

steps.title: Transfer cell pellet to cryo-safe sample bags
steps.instruction: Transfer cell pellet to cryo-safe sample bags. Mark with full and clear tracking information.

steps.title: Store cell pellet -80 °C
steps.instruction: Store cell pellet -80 °C

steps.title: Transfer 1 ml of culture sample...
steps.instruction: Transfer 1 ml of culture sample to a microcentrifuge tube go to step #8.2

steps.title: Add 100 µL, lysis reagent to...
steps.instruction: Add 100 µL, lysis reagent to the sample If biomass is low or protein expression levels are expected to be low, take a larger culture sample and pellet it first. This will increase the target protein yield but you may need to check sufficient lysis has occurred.

steps.title: Using Nickel Magnetic Beads perform a rapid over-expression assay
steps.instruction: Using Nickel Magnetic Beads perform a rapid over-expression assay. The protocol described here is abridged and achieves only incomplete cell lysis, but can be used to varify over-expression in 5-10 minutes, plus gel running time.

steps.title: Incubate Room temperature 00:02:00, in a tube rotor
steps.instruction: Incubate Room temperature 00:02:00, in a tube rotor

steps.title: Aspirate soluble fraction ( mixed proteins ) with pipette
steps.instruction: Aspirate soluble fraction ( mixed proteins ) with pipette

steps.title: Add 50 µL, magnetic IMAC beads...
steps.instruction: Add 50 µL, magnetic IMAC beads to the soluble fraction ( mixed proteins )

steps.title: Incubate Room temperature 00:05:00, in a tube rotor
steps.instruction: Incubate Room temperature 00:05:00, in a tube rotor

steps.title: Incubate Room temperature 00:01:00, in a magnetic separator stand
steps.instruction: Incubate Room temperature 00:01:00, in a magnetic separator stand

steps.title: Aspirate soluble fraction ( unbound material...
steps.instruction: Aspirate soluble fraction ( unbound material ), reserve for SDS-PAGE analysis.

steps.title: Wash the retained beads 2 mL, binding buffer 00:01:00
steps.instruction: Wash the retained beads 2 mL, binding buffer 00:01:00

steps.title: Incubate Room temperature 00:01:00, in a magnetic separator stand
steps.instruction: Incubate Room temperature 00:01:00, in a magnetic separator stand

steps.title: Aspirate soluble fraction ( unbound material...
steps.instruction: Aspirate soluble fraction ( unbound material ), reserve for SDS-PAGE analysis.

steps.title: Add 50 µL, elution buffer to the mag beads
steps.instruction: Add 50 µL, elution buffer to the mag beads

steps.title: Aspirate soluble fraction ( eluted protein ) with pipette
steps.instruction: Aspirate soluble fraction ( eluted protein ) with pipette

steps.title: Measure the A280 using a spectrophotometer (e
steps.instruction: Measure the A280 using a spectrophotometer (e.g. NanoDrop)

steps.title: Run an SDS-PAGE gel, to check...
steps.instruction: Run an SDS-PAGE gel, to check for over-expression and quality control

steps.title: Tracking IDs for internal use and yields
steps.instruction: Tracking IDs for internal use and yields: A B C D E F G 1 Construct ID Expression ID Culture Total Volume (L) OD600 Pellet wet cell weight (g) SDS-Page: Test purification mag prep (pass) Expression growth (pass) 2 XX01ZVNS2B-c002 XX01ZVNS2B-e006 9 17.6 202.1 Pass Pass ZIKV and DENV-2 NS2B-NS3 protease co-expressed IDs and yields.

steps.title: If cultures are innoculated by late...
steps.instruction: If cultures are innoculated by late morning biomass growth can be monitored in person. If choosing to manually induce protein expression by IPTG this can be done before the end of the work day. Expression can run unattended overnight.

steps.title: A typical culture, having been innoculated...
steps.instruction: A typical culture, having been innoculated at between 10-12 pm on day 1, can be harvested in the morning of day 2. This gives a total culture time of 20-24 hours. Culture times of up to 36 hours have been tried successfully, but this is likely to be cell/protein specific. Short expression experiments can be performed with a c.3 hour induction period following the addition of IPTG. Culture biomass can be measured by taking Optical Density readings at 600 nm in a spectrophotometer. The relationship between OD600 and biomass is non-linear at high concentrations. If using a fixed path-length or cuvette, dilute samples such that readings remain below 1 OD unit. If using autoinduction media and cell growth appears to have stalled below OD600=5, IPTG can be added to ensure induction has occurred.

steps.title: In rich media biomass will continue to increase after induction
steps.instruction: In rich media biomass will continue to increase after induction. Once OD600 values plateau late log phase has occurred, and cell harvest must occur before OD600 begins to drop when death phase begins.

steps.title: Annotated SDS-PAGE gel of Mag Prep...
steps.instruction: Annotated SDS-PAGE gel of Mag Prep test purifications of co-expressed ZIKV and DENV-2 NS2B-NS3 protease.

steps.title: Bag setup
steps.instruction: Bag setup: a) Guidance for Bag manufacture and assembly, b)Arrangement of the Bags over the hanging rail, c) A Bag Array in operation. Bag set-up a) Bags removed from water bath for illustrative purposes, b) atypical bubble swarm and foam layer, c) close-up of the Bags in the water bathwith heaters, d) a heat map showing water bath heating of the Bags;left-to-right Bags acclimating.

steps.title: The bag with airline inserted, and...
steps.instruction: The bag with airline inserted, and a funnel being used to demonstrate the filling process.

steps.title: Incubate 00:02:00 Room temperature
steps.instruction: Incubate 00:02:00 Room temperature

steps.title: Incubate 00:01:00, in a magnetic separator stand
steps.instruction: Incubate 00:01:00, in a magnetic separator stand

steps.title: Pellet the cell debris at maximum...
steps.instruction: Pellet the cell debris at maximum speed in a microtube centrifuge 16000 rpm, °C, 2 minutes There is no need to spin at 4 °C unless you plan to use the eluted protein for other purposes than QC SDS-PAGE.
